document.title: IEC 60601-1:2005+AMD1:2012+AMD2:2020 CSV
document.documentType: International medical electrical equipment safety standard

sourceSummary.oneLine: This chunk defines core IEC 60601-1 safety terms and states general safety, risk management, classification, testing, power supply, and marking requirements for ME equipment and ME systems.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Use as regulatory wiki evidence for IEC 60601-1 terminology and compliance expectations on risk management, essential performance, type testing, single fault safety, component qualification, power input, classification, and marking legibility/durability.
keyPoints: IEC 60601-1 requires a risk management process complying with ISO 14971, with specified exclusions for production/post-production monitoring and periodic process review.
Manufacturers must identify essential performance, state expected service life, justify alternative risk controls, and document applicable risk management evidence.
ME equipment classification covers electric shock protection class, applied part type, ingress protection, sterilization method, oxygen-rich environment suitability, and operation mode.
Testing requirements include type tests, representative samples, least favourable conditions, humidity preconditioning, accessible part testing, and marking legibility/durability tests.

entities.name: International Electrotechnical Commission
entities.evidence: Headers identify the document as IEC 60601-1:2005+AMD1:2012+AMD2:2020 CSV and copyright IEC 2020.

entities.name: Manufacturer
entities.evidence: Manufacturer is defined as the person responsible for design, manufacture, packaging, labelling, assembling an ME system, or adapting ME equipment or an ME system.

entities.name: Responsible Organization
entities.evidence: Responsible Organization is defined as the entity accountable for the use and maintenance of ME equipment or an ME system.

entities.name: Operator
entities.evidence: Operator is defined as a person handling equipment.

entities.name: Service Personnel
entities.evidence: Service Personnel are individuals or entities accountable to the Responsible Organization that install, assemble, maintain, or repair ME equipment, ME systems, or equipment.

entities.name: ISO 14971:2019
entities.evidence: Clause 4.2 states that a risk management process complying with ISO 14971 shall be performed.

concepts.name: Basic Safety
concepts.evidence: Basic Safety is freedom from unacceptable risk directly caused by physical hazards under normal condition and single fault condition.

concepts.name: Essential Performance
concepts.evidence: Essential Performance is performance necessary to achieve freedom from unacceptable risk; the manufacturer shall identify which functions are essential performance.

concepts.name: Medical Electrical Equipment
concepts.evidence: ME equipment is electrical equipment having an applied part, transferring energy to/from the patient, or detecting such transfer, and intended for diagnosis, treatment, monitoring, compensation, or alleviation.

concepts.name: Medical Electrical System
concepts.evidence: ME system is a manufacturer-specified combination of equipment items, at least one being ME equipment, interconnected by functional connection or multiple socket-outlet.

concepts.name: Risk Management File
concepts.evidence: Risk Management File is a set of records and other documents produced by a risk management process; safety information including calculations and test results is part of the file.

concepts.name: Single Fault Safe
concepts.evidence: Single Fault Safe means ME equipment remains free of unacceptable risk during expected service life under single fault conditions.

concepts.name: Applied Part Type Classification
concepts.evidence: Applied parts are classified as Type B, Type BF, or Type CF applied parts; defibrillation-proof applied parts may also be classified.

concepts.name: Type Test
concepts.evidence: A type test is performed on a representative sample to determine whether equipment as designed and manufactured can meet this standard.

concepts.name: Marking Legibility and Durability
concepts.evidence: Required markings must be clearly legible and sufficiently durable to remain clearly legible during expected service life.

evidence.claim: IEC 60601-1 requirements apply to ME equipment and ME systems in normal use and reasonably foreseeable misuse unless otherwise specified.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 4.1 states that, unless otherwise specified, requirements apply in NORMAL USE and reasonably foreseeable misuse.

evidence.claim: IEC 60601-1 requires performance of a risk management process complying with ISO 14971.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 4.2 states: A RISK MANAGEMENT PROCESS complying with ISO 14971 shall be performed.

evidence.claim: For IEC 60601-1 risk management, ISO 14971's term medical device is interpreted as ME equipment or ME system.
evidence.evidenceText: When applying ISO 14971, the term medical device shall assume the same meaning as ME EQUIPMENT or ME SYSTEM.

evidence.claim: For IEC 60601-1 risk management, ISO 14971 fault conditions include at least the single fault conditions identified in IEC 60601-1.
evidence.evidenceText: The term fault conditions referred to in ISO 14971 shall include, but shall not be limited to, SINGLE FAULT CONDITIONS identified in this standard.

evidence.claim: The manufacturer establishes acceptable risk policy and acceptability of residual risks for IEC 60601-1 compliance.
evidence.evidenceText: The policy for determining acceptable RISK and acceptability of RESIDUAL RISK(S) shall be established by the MANUFACTURER.

evidence.claim: IEC 60601-1 risk management compliance is checked by inspecting risk acceptability policy, risk management plan, and risk management file.
evidence.evidenceText: Compliance is checked by inspection of the manufacturer's policy, inspection of the risk management plan, and confirming preparation of a risk management file.

evidence.claim: IEC 60601-1 does not require all ISO 14971:2019 elements; production/post-production monitoring and periodic suitability reviews are excluded for compliance with this standard.
evidence.evidenceText: All ISO 14971:2019 risk management elements apply except planning/execution of production and post-production monitoring and periodic reviews of process suitability.

evidence.claim: Where IEC 60601-series requirements address hazards with acceptance criteria, compliance is presumed to reduce residual risks to acceptable levels unless objective evidence shows otherwise.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 4.2.3.1(a) states compliance with requirements addressing hazards and specific acceptance criteria is presumed to establish acceptable residual risks unless objective evidence to the contrary exists.

evidence.claim: If IEC 60601-series requirements address hazards without specific acceptance criteria, the manufacturer must provide acceptance criteria in the risk management plan.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 4.2.3.1(b) requires manufacturer-defined acceptance criteria in the risk management plan where the standard addresses hazards but gives no specific acceptance criteria.

evidence.claim: Hazards not specifically addressed by IEC 60601-1 or collateral/particular standards must be handled through the risk management process.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 4.2.3.2 requires hazards or hazardous situations not specifically addressed by IEC 60601 standards to be addressed in the risk management process.

evidence.claim: Manufacturers must identify essential performance functions and evaluate loss or degradation beyond specified limits through risk analysis.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 4.3 requires the manufacturer to identify essential performance, specify performance limits, and evaluate risk from loss or degradation beyond those limits.

evidence.claim: When IEC 60601-1 requires essential performance to be maintained after a test, compliance is checked by inspection and, if necessary, functional testing.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 4.3 states compliance is checked by inspection and, if necessary, functional tests demonstrating specified limits are maintained or equipment transitions to a manufacturer-defined safe state.

evidence.claim: The manufacturer must state expected service life of ME equipment or ME system in the risk management file.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 4.4 states the manufacturer shall state expected service life in the risk management file; compliance is checked by inspection.

evidence.claim: Alternative risk control measures or test methods are acceptable only if justified as producing comparable acceptable residual risk.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 4.5 permits alternatives where the manufacturer demonstrates through scientific data, clinical opinion, or comparative studies that residual risk remains acceptable and comparable.

evidence.claim: Parts contacting the patient but outside the applied part definition must be assessed by risk management to determine whether applied part requirements apply.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 4.6 requires risk management assessment of patient-contacting parts outside the applied part definition, with relevant applied part requirements and tests applied if needed.

evidence.claim: ME equipment must be designed and manufactured to remain single fault safe or have acceptable risk under IEC 60601-1 risk management.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 4.7 states ME equipment shall remain SINGLE FAULT SAFE, or risk shall remain acceptable as determined through application of 4.2.

evidence.claim: During single fault condition testing, only one fault at a time is applied, and risk analysis determines which failures are tested.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 4.7 states only one fault at a time shall be applied, and results of risk analysis determine which failures shall be tested.

evidence.claim: Components whose failure could create a hazardous situation must be used within specified ratings unless IEC 60601-1 or risk management permits an exception.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 4.8 requires all components, including wiring, whose failure could result in a hazardous situation to be used according to specified ratings unless excepted.

evidence.claim: Components used as means of protection must have reliability assessed for their use conditions and comply with applicable IEC/ISO safety requirements or IEC 60601-1 requirements.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 4.8 requires assessment of component reliability for ME equipment use conditions and compliance with applicable IEC/ISO component safety requirements or this standard.

evidence.claim: High-integrity components are required when a fault in a component can generate unacceptable risk.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 4.9 states a component with high-integrity characteristics shall be used when a fault in a particular component can generate unacceptable risk.

evidence.claim: ME equipment must be suitable for supply mains, specified separate power supply, internal electrical power source, or a combination.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 4.10.1 requires ME equipment to be suitable for connection to supply mains, specified for separate power supply, internally powered, or a combination.

evidence.claim: Rated supply voltages for ME equipment connected to supply mains are limited by equipment type and input power.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 4.10.2 limits rated voltages to 250 V for hand-held ME equipment; 250 V dc/single-phase ac or 500 V polyphase ac for input ≤4 kVA; and 500 V for all other ME equipment and systems.

evidence.claim: Measured steady-state input must not exceed marked rating by more than 10 percent at rated voltage and operating settings indicated in the instructions for use.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 4.11 states steady-state measured input at rated voltage and indicated operating settings shall not exceed marked rating by more than 10 percent.

evidence.claim: IEC 60601-1 tests are type tests, and risk analysis can determine whether tests or combinations of simultaneous faults are needed.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 5.1 states tests are type tests, tests are determined considering Clause 4, and risk analysis determines combinations of simultaneous faults to be tested.

evidence.claim: Type tests are performed on representative samples of the item being tested.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 5.2 states type tests are performed on a representative sample, with multiple samples allowed if validity is not significantly affected.

evidence.claim: ME equipment is generally tested under least favourable working conditions identified during risk analysis and specified in accompanying documents.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 5.4 requires testing under least favourable working conditions specified in instructions for use and identified during risk analysis.

evidence.claim: Humidity preconditioning is required before specified leakage current and dielectric strength tests, with 93 percent ±3 percent relative humidity conditions.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 5.7 requires humidity preconditioning before tests of 8.7.4 and 8.8.3 in a cabinet at 93 percent ±3 percent relative humidity.

evidence.claim: Applied parts are identified by inspection and by reference to accompanying documents.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 5.9.1 states applied parts are identified by inspection and reference to the accompanying documents.

evidence.claim: Accessible parts are identified by inspection and, where necessary, test with a standard test finger.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 5.9.2.1 requires accessible parts to be identified by inspection and, in case of doubt, by a standard test finger applied in bent or straight position.

evidence.claim: ME equipment and applied parts must be classified for electric shock protection, including Class I, Class II, internally powered, and Type B/BF/CF applied parts.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 6.2 requires externally powered ME equipment to be Class I or Class II, other equipment internally powered, and applied parts classified as Type B, BF, or CF.

evidence.claim: Enclosures must be classified for harmful ingress of water and particulate matter according to IEC 60529.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 6.3 states enclosures shall be classified according to the degree of protection against harmful ingress of water and particulate matter as detailed in IEC 60529.

evidence.claim: ME equipment or parts intended to be sterilized must be classified by sterilization method as indicated in the instructions for use.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 6.4 requires classification according to sterilization methods indicated in instructions for use, with examples including ethylene oxide, irradiation, moist heat, and validated manufacturer methods.

evidence.claim: ME equipment and systems intended for oxygen-rich environments must be classified for such use.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 6.5 states ME equipment and ME systems intended for use in an oxygen-rich environment shall be classified for such use.

evidence.claim: ME equipment must be classified for continuous or non-continuous operation.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 6.6 states ME equipment shall be classified for either continuous operation or non-continuous operation.

evidence.claim: Manufacturers must address poor usability risk associated with identification, marking, and documents through a usability engineering process.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 7.1.1 requires the manufacturer to address poor usability risk for identification, marking, and documents in a usability engineering process.

evidence.claim: Required markings must be clearly legible under specified viewing, position, lighting, and observer acuity conditions.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 7.1.2 requires markings in 7.2-7.6 to be clearly legible and specifies viewpoint, illuminance range of 100 lx to 1 500 lx, and observer acuity criteria.

evidence.claim: Required markings must be durable enough to remain clearly legible during expected service life.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 7.1.3 requires markings to be removable only with a tool or appreciable force and durable through expected service life; durability includes rubbing tests with water, ethanol, and isopropyl alcohol.

reviewFlags.reason: This is chunk 2 of 13; extracted facts are limited to definitions and requirements present in this character range.
The chunk appears to contain IEC consolidated amendment markup/OCR artifacts, including duplicated old and amended text; evidence was normalized only where the regulatory meaning is clear.
